response.justification: Bogdan has a broad full-stack background and genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective, but the role's core requirement — production-grade C++ or Rust for routing data pipelines — is a significant gap. His C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project; there is no Rust experience at all. His geospatial work is data-collection oriented (NGO mapping) rather than routing tile optimization or navigation pipeline engineering. The distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby is relevant in spirit but was implemented in DLang, not C++. Without demonstrated proficiency in C++ or Rust and no routing/navigation domain experience, the candidate does not meet the minimum bar for this role.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform used in production by Green Map)
Distributed systems and high-throughput experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency design
Strong Linux background across multiple roles and side projects, including kernel-adjacent work (Jack audio, C++ Sound Position Server)
concerns: No meaningful C++ experience since 2015 (~6 months at NI) and zero Rust experience — both are the primary technical requirement
Geospatial work is data collection and visualization, not routing tile generation, pipeline optimization, or navigation accuracy
Current and recent work (2018–present) is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js; a pivot to systems-level C++ routing pipelines would be a large reorientation
